etymology.description: 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear") + phonetic 殸(殸) -> 聲(聲)
etymology.interpretation: The character 聲(聲) (shēng, "sound; voice") combines the semantic indicator 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear") to represent the idea of sound heard by the ear, with the phonetic component 殸(殸) providing the sound element. This reflects the essential nature of sound as something that is perceived audibly through the ears.
etymology.simplification: 声(聲) is the simplified form where 殸 was replaced with 士(士) (shì, "scholar") and 工(工) (gōng, "work") was omitted; it retains the core meaning through phonetic reduction.

examples.chinese: 那是一种美妙的声音(那是一種美妙的聲音)。
examples.pinyin: Nà shì yī zhǒng měimiào de shēngyīn.

examples.chinese: 我听到了他的声音(我聽到了他的聲音)，感到很高兴(感到很高興)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ tīngdào le tā de shēngyīn, gǎndào hěn gāoxìng.
examples.english: I heard his voice and felt very happy.

examples.chinese: 风的声音很柔和(風的聲音很柔和)。
examples.english: The sound of the wind is gentle.
